commands.author: ooiuuii

target.title: test(scripts): update helper route expectations
target.body:
## Summary
- Update the `test/helpers/temp-dir.ts` route expectation in `src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`.
- Include the current helper importers that now resolve through the tooling Vitest project.
- Keep the change test-only; no runtime routing behavior changes.

## What Problem This Solves
`checks-node-core-tooling` failed on #94084 after rebasing onto current main because `buildVitestRunPlans(["test/helpers/temp-dir.ts"])` returned additional importer tests that were not listed in the expected tooling include patterns.

The route fixture had drifted behind current test imports, so the tooling shard failed even though the helper routing behavior was correctly discovering the new importers.

## Linked context
- Found from #94084 CI after rebasing it onto current main.
- This PR is intentionally separate from #94084 because the failure is a current-main test fixture drift, not plugin update behavior.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: The `test-projects args > routes top-level test helpers to importing repo tests` assertion failed because the expected tooling include list omitted current `test/helpers/temp-dir.ts` importers.
- Real environment tested: Windows PowerShell checkout at `F:\openclaw\worktrees\openclaw-test-projects-helper-routes`, hydrated with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts --testNamePattern="routes top-level test helpers to importing repo tests"`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 F:/openclaw/worktrees/openclaw-test-projects-helper-routes

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 82 skipped (83)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.08s
```

- Observed result after fix: `buildVitestRunPlans(["test/helpers/temp-dir.ts"])` now matches the full current importer set, including `config-reload-mutate-metadata`, `e2e-text-file-utils`, `fixture-plugin-commands`, `incremental-line-reader`, and `onboard-config-fixtures`.
- What was not tested: I did not rerun the full `checks-node-core-tooling` shard locally; this PR targets the exact failing assertion from that shard.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a test fixture repair. It does not change persistent data models, embeddings, runtime state, or production code.
- Before evidence:

```text
FAIL src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts > test-projects args > routes top-level test helpers to importing repo tests
AssertionError: expected ... to deeply equal ...
+ "test/scripts/config-reload-mutate-metadata.test.ts"
+ "test/scripts/e2e-text-file-utils.test.ts"
+ "test/scripts/fixture-plugin-commands.test.ts"
+ "test/scripts/incremental-line-reader.test.ts"
+ "test/scripts/onboard-config-fixtures.test.ts"
```

## Evidence
Before this patch, the focused assertion failed because current helper importer tests were missing from the expected route list.

After this patch, the focused `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --testNamePattern="routes top-level test helpers to importing repo tests"` command passes with `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 1 passed | 82 skipped (83)`.

## Tests and validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts --testNamePattern="routes top-level test helpers to importing repo tests"`
- `git diff --check`

## Risk checklist
- [x] Test-only fixture update.
- [x] No runtime behavior changes.
- [x] No persistent data model, embedding, migration, config, security, auth, network, or tool execution impact.
- [x] Highest risk is over-including unrelated importers; mitigated by the focused assertion run against the current discovered route list.

## Current review state
Ready for ClawSweeper re-review and maintainer review.
target.author: ooiuuii
